text: fat is 11.8g. cholesterol is also part of the tradeoff. for meats, fat quality is one of the biggest separators once protein is already solid.
carbs is 0g. everything else is lackluster. for meats, carbs are barely the point, so the rest has to justify the food.
protein is 26.1g. protein quality is clearly one of the selling points. for meats, strong protein is expected, so the side factors decide how high it climbs.
vitamin b12 at 100% DV, vitamin d at 0% DV stand out most. that gives this food real vitamin support.
zinc at 39% DV, iron at 15% DV, potassium at 9% DV stand out most. that stays fairly limited overall.

the upsides first. high satiety protein source because that is a major real-world strength for meat. complete protein delivery is reliable because the category is supposed to do this well. easy to centre a meal around because practical meal value matters.

on the weaker side. fat quality can go sideways fast because some meats are strong until the fat profile shows up. cost and storage can be a pain because practical downsides still count. brings no fibre support because that leaves a nutrition gap the rest of the meal has to cover.
Lean Beef is B tier. Best when you want efficient protein and can accept the category tradeoffs that come with it.
